reasoning: The FRED statistical trend and seasonal analysis establishes a 63% probability that the rate will increase above its current level by the first resolution date (Dec 14). I am using this 0.63 as my primary anchor for the short-term forecast. For the medium and longer-term resolution dates (Jan 6 and Mar 7, 2026), macroeconomic outlooks (including recent Fannie Mae forecasts) project mortgage rates to gradually fall into the high 5% to low 6% range in 2026 as the Fed is expected to cut interest rates. Consequently, the probability of an increase relative to the December 2025 baseline begins to decline. Following the explicit instruction that uncertainty should increase and probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5, I have regressed the probabilities for Jan 6 and Mar 7 progressively closer to 50%, crossing slightly into probable-decrease territory by March 2026 to reflect the widely anticipated downward drift in mortgage rates over the medium-term.
